{% extends "base.html" %} {% block title %}{{ config.title }} — documentation{% endblock title %} {% block content %}
Documentation

soma‑schema

Plain SQL database migrations for Rust — manifest-defined ordering, full-file drift detection, and run-scoped advisory locking.

→ Quickstart Installation
Manifest ordering
Explicit order. Deterministic rollback.
migration-order.yaml defines apply and rollback order explicitly — not filename sort. FK-safe rollback without naming conventions.
Drift detection
Full-file SHA-256 checksums.
The checksum covers UP and DOWN together. Editing the DOWN section of a deployed migration is caught as ChecksumDrift on next run.
Atomic apply
Apply and track in one transaction.
Migration SQL and its tracking row commit atomically. A crash between those two operations leaves no split state.
Advisory lock
Run-scoped Postgres lock.
One advisory lock held for the entire up/down call via a RAII guard. Concurrent runners block rather than collide.
{{ section.content | safe }}
{% endblock content %}
